parts_list.description: Lift & Console Conduit Locations, Section Views

notes: Most often used for fleet maintenance, repair and service applications
Lift unit translates to the rear approximately 36 1/8 during vertical articulation
Use smooth electrical 90's in conduits, not plumbing 90's
Materials shown on this list shall be used without substitution unless specifically approved in writing by Mohawk Resources, Ltd.
Units supplied by Mohawk with the lift unit marked with *

pages_summary.description: Lift data table and required material list
pages_summary.extracted_text: LIFT DATA TABLE, MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD, PARALLELOGRAM LIFT MODEL 50-26-SURFACE, LIFT UNIT DATA, MAXIMUM LOAD CAPACITY (LBS) 50,000 (22680 kg), ANCHORAGE, ANCHOR BOLT DIAMETER (IN.) 3/4 (19), TOTAL NUMBER OF ANCHOR BOLTS 45, BOLT PATTERN 9, ANCHOR BOLT SETTING TORQUE 6, MINIMUM EMBEDMENT LENGTH (IN.) SEE ANCHOR DETAILS, MINIMUM CONCRETE THICKNESS (IN.) 8 (152) ON GRADE (SEE NOTE 25), HYDRAULIC, RESERVOIR CAPACITY 30 GALLONS (114 LITERS), OIL TYPE DEXRON III ATF, ELECTRICAL, MOTOR HORSEPOWER 20, 208/230, 1 PH 60 AMPERE, 460V, 3 PH 30 AMPERE, CONTROL CIRCUIT TRANSFORMER 1000 VA, 24 VDC POWER SUPPLY 4.8 AMP, LIGHT FIXTURES (OPTIONAL LIGHTING KIT) QTY 6, SHOP AIR, AIR PRESSURE 85 to 100 PSI (586 TO 690 Pa), AIR VOLUME— LIFT LOCKS 5 CFM (.636 cm³/MIN), AIR VOLUME— OPTIONAL ROLLING JACK (CFM) 25 EACH, AIR VOLUME— OPTIONAL SHOP AIR KIT (CFM) 30 MINIMUM, AIR VOLUME— TOTAL REQ'D CAPACITY (CFM) 50 SUGGESTED, REQUIRED MATERIAL LIST, MATERIALS SHOWN ON THIS LIST SHALL BE USED WITHOUT SUBSTITUTION UNLESS SPECIFICALLY APPROVED IN WRITING BY MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD., ITEM QTY DESCRIPTION, LOCKOUT/TAGOUT DISCONNECT BOX, LEVELING SHIMS, 3/4 (19) x 5 (127) ANCHOR BOLT ASSEMBLY, 1 (25.4) SEAL BARRIER, 1—3/4 x 8 (44) WEDGE BUSHING, 10 (254) SCH 40-90 DEG ELBOW, JUNCTION BOX, SEALING FLUID CONDUIT, 1/2 LIQUID TIGHT CONDUIT, FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR, DRYER SHUTOFF, 4 (102) SCH 40 STREET ELBOW, 4 (102) SCH 40 TEE, DESCRIPTION, * ITEMS SUPPLIED BY MOHAWK WITH THE LIFT UNIT, UNITS = INCH (mm) = POUNDS (kg)

GENERAL NOTES, NOTE 1, IF THE EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A DOCUMENTED MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c= 4,000 PSI (28 MPa) AND IS NOT LESS THAN SIX INCHES (152) THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE USED WITHOUT ALTERATIONS. GENERALLY ANY FLOOR AREA WHICH IS DESIGNED FOR VEHICLES OF THE SAME WEIGHT AS THE LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY WILL BE ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT. LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY DATA IS PROVIDED IN THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE, NOTE 2, IF THE CONCRETE FLOOR SYSTEM DOES NOT MEET MINIMUM SPECIFICATIONS OF NOTE 1 ABOVE, THEN A NEW CONCRETE SYSTEM SHALL BE INSTALLED TO SUPPORT THE LIFT, NOTE 3, IF THE STRENGTH OF AN EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM IS UNKNOWN OR NOT DOCUMENTED, ITS STRENGTH SHALL BE DETERMINED. CORE SAMPLES SHOULD BE TAKEN TO DETERMINE THE STRENGTH OF THE FLOOR, NOTE 4, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM SHALL BE AT A MINIMUM F'c=4,000 psi (27.6 MPa), WITH HEAVY AGGREGATE. FOR ANY NEW CONCRETE IT SHALL REACH ITS DESIGN 28 DAY CURE STRENGTH BEFORE THE LIFT AND THE LIFT ANCHORS ARE INSTALLED, NOTE 5, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR FOOTINGS, PADS OR ALTERATIONS SHALL HAVE REINFORCING AS RECOMMENDED BY OTHERS. FOOTINGS AND PADS SHALL CONFORM TO LOCAL BUILDING CODES, CONFORMING OR OTHER, OF A MINIMUM TWO LAYERS OF #4 REBAR 3 INCHES ON CENTER. WELDED WIRE FABRIC SHALL BE USED FOR ANY FLOOR REPAIRS. ALSO, FLOOR REPAIRS SHALL BE DOWELED INTO THE EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM TO PREVENT DIFFERENTIAL SETTLEMENT, NOTE 6, FOR INSTALLATION IN EXISTING STRUCTURES, AREAS OF THE FLOOR WHICH ARE CUT AND REMOVED FOR SERVICE CONDUIT INSTALLATIONS SHALL BE REPAIRED WITH CONCRETE HAVING MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN 4,000 psi (27.6 MPa), WITH AT LEAST 3 INCHES (229) THICK, INTO THE AREAS GROUND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS, NOTE 7, FOR NEW CONSTRUCTION, THE AREAS OF THE FLOOR ALONG THE LIFT RUNWAYS SHOULD BE DEEPENED TO 8 INCHES (203) FOR $8 PER 500 ANCHOR INSTALLATION. ALSO, PROVIDE A MINIMUM OF 1/2 INCH (228) THICKNESS AROUND THE BASE OF LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEGS, NOTE 8, FOR CONSTRUCTION WHERE IN SLAB FLOOR HEATING TUBES ARE USED, THESE TUBES MAY BE PLACED UNDER THE LIFT AREA PROVIDED THE SLAB IS CAST SUFFICIENTLY THICK. A MINIMUM OF 6 INCHES (152) OF CONCRETE SHOULD BE PROVIDED OVER ANCHOR BOLTS AND DRILLING FOR ALIGNMENT. THE INSTALLER SHALL LOCATE THE TUBES PRIOR TO ANCHOR DRILLING TO PREVENT RUPTURE OF THE DEPTH OF THE ANCHORS, NOTE 9, THE SUPPORT PLATES OF THE CONTINUOUS BASE SHALL NOT BE INSTALLED OVER A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OF THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE SUPPORT BASE PLATES SHALL NOT BE PLACED NEARER THAN 10 INCHES (254) TO A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OR FREE EDGE OF THE FLOOR SLAB, NOTE 10, A MAXIMUM OF ONE INCH (25.4) ANCHOR BOLT SHIM THICKNESS IS PERMITTED. INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE IN A RANGE OF THICKNESSES, NOTE 11, WHERE MORE THAN ONE INCH (25.4) OF SHIM LEVELING IS REQUIRED FULL SUPPORT PLATE CONTACT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE AT ADDITIONAL LEVELED COST. FULL CONTACT SHIM PLATES SHALL THEN BE ACCOMPLISHED BY EPOXY USING INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS, NOTE 12, WEJ-IT FASTENING SYSTEMS, AT WEDGE ANCHORS ARE PROVIDED WITH THE LIFT FOR ANCHORING THE LIFT UNIT TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE NUMBER AND THE SIZE OF ANCHOR BOLTS SPECIFIED IN THE DRAWING MUST BE USED TO ATTACH THE LIFT UNIT. ANCHOR BOLTS OF FULL LENGTH MUST BE USED IN ALL LOCATIONS PROVIDED ON THE BASE OF THE LIFT UNIT, SURFACE LIFTS ONLY, UNITS = INCH (mm), NOTE 12, IN CERTAIN CASES THE FLOOR SLAB MAY HAVE ADEQUATE STRENGTH TO SUPPORT THE LIFT UNIT BUT MAY NOT BE THICK ENOUGH TO PROVIDE THE MINIMUM EMBEDMENT DEPTH FOR THE AT TYPICAL ANCHOR BOLTS. IN CASES SUCH AS THESE SHORTER ANCHOR BOLTS MAY BE USED CONTACT MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD. FOR WRITTEN APPROVAL OF EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS FOR LIFT APPLICATIONS AND APPROPRIATE INSTALLATION FOR LIFT UNIT, NOTE 13, EXCEPT AS DESCRIBED IN NOTE 7, NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, EXCEPT THE LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE CLOSER THAN 16 INCHES (406) FROM ANY ANCHOR BOLT. ALSO, ALL SERVICE LEG CONDUITS MUST BE INSTALLED ACCURATELY IN THE LOCATIONS SHOWN IN THE PLAN AND DETAIL VIEWS TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECT ON THE ANCHORAGE, NOTE 14, NO ANCHOR BOLT SHALL BE INSTALLED CLOSER THAN 10 INCHES (254) FROM ANY FREE EDGE OR FLOOR JOINT, NOTE 15, PROVIDE 4 INCH (102) SCH 40 PVC PIPE AS A HYDRAULIC—PNEUMATIC SERVICE SUPPLY CONDUIT RUNNING FROM POWER UNIT TO EACH SERVICE LEG, NOTE 16, PROVIDE 4, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 STEEL CONDUITS AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE CONTROL TO EACH SERVICE LEG. THESE CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED AS SHOWN ON THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS AND MUST BE IN THE PRECISE LOCATIONS SPECIFIED TO ACCOMMODATE, NOTE 17, PROVIDE TEMPORARY CAPS FOR ALL CONDUITS AND EMBEDDED PIPES. IT IS RECOMMENDED TO LEAVE PULL ROPES IN CONDUITS FOR EASE OF LIFT INSTALLATION, NOTE 18, THE CONTROL CONSOLE MUST BE LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT. IT SHOULD BE PLACED FAR ENOUGH FROM THE LIFT TO ALLOW FOR VEHICLE LOADING THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS SHOW THE CONTROL CONSOLE IN A STANDARD POSITION. THE CONTROL CONSOLE MAY BE LOCATED ON EITHER SIDE AND ANYWHERE ALONG THE LENGTH OF THE LIFT, BUT ANY DEVIATIONS FROM THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS MAY REQUIRE LONGER CABLES, HOSES, CONDUIT, ETC. ADDITIONAL EXPENSE TO THE PURCHASER, NOTE 19, THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES A HIGH VOLTAGE POWER SOURCE. A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE PROVIDED FOR POWER SOURCE. THE LOCKOUT/TAGOUT DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES. THIS ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT IS TO BE PROVIDED BY OTHERS, NOTE 20, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE MAIN ELECTRICAL PANEL TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE. THIS CONDUIT MUST BE RUN UNDERGROUND ALTERNATELY IT MAY BE BROUGHT TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. PROVIDE A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX AND WIRE AS CLOSE TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE AS IS PRACTICAL. THIS ELECTRICAL SUPPLY CONDUIT AND DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO LOCAL ELECTRICAL CODE REQUIREMENTS, NOTE 21, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS A COMPRESSED AIR SUPPLY. THIS CONDUIT IS SHOWN UNDERGROUND, ALTERNATIVELY IT MAY BE BROUGHT TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD, DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. IF BROUGHT OVERHEAD, PROVIDE FLEX CONDUIT CONNECTING THE TERMINAL END OF THE CONDUIT TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE, NOTE 22, THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES CLEAN DRY COMPRESSED AIR AT THE PRESSURE AND VOLUME SHOWN ON THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE. A FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR IS SUPPLIED WITH THE LIFT UNIT FOR THE LIFT. UNIT OWNER IS RESPONSIBLE FOR PROVIDING MAIN SUPPLY AIR SUITABLE FOR THE UNIT SUPPLIED FOR THE LIFT UNIT TO OPERATE THE OPTIONAL ACCESSORIES. THE REQUIRED VOLUME OF AIR SHOWN IN THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE RECOMMENDS THAT MORE THAN ONE AUXILIARY AIR CONSUMER WILL BE USED SIMULTANEOUSLY, NOTE 23, ALL FLOOR REQUIREMENTS ARE BASED ON A CONCRETE SLAB THAT IS ON GRADE (SUPPORTED BY SOIL). ANY OTHER TYPE OF INSTALLATION INVOLVING A SLAB NOT ON GRADE (I.E.—SLAB SUPPORTED BY PYLONS, SECOND STORY SLAB, ETC.) MUST BE REVIEWED & ANALYZED FOR SUITABILITY BY THE BUILDING ENGINEER OR ARCHITECT OF RECORD BY OTHERS, MOHAWK RESOURCES LTD., SURFACE INSTALLATION GENERAL NOTES
